title: Attachment Style
description: Discover your attachment style in relationships. Choose the most honest answer that describes you.

questions.question: When your partner doesn't reply to your message immediately, you...

options.text: It's fine, they must be busy
Worry, overthink, and check your phone repeatedly
Don't care, you also rarely reply quickly

questions.question: An ideal partner in your opinion is someone who...

options.text: Gives freedom and balanced trust
Is always there, responsive, and gives full attention
Isn't too demanding and highly respects privacy

questions.question: When facing a conflict with your partner, you prefer to...

options.text: Discuss the problem calmly until it's resolved
Be very emotional, cry, and want to make up right away
Withdraw, give the silent treatment, and need time alone

questions.question: How do you feel when you're single?

options.text: Comfortable, I can enjoy my time alone well
Restless, lonely, and feel like something is missing in life
Very relieved, I prefer freedom over being tied down

questions.question: When someone tries to get too emotionally close to you, you...

options.text: Feel happy, that process feels natural to build a connection
Get very enthusiastic, you are hungry for closeness and validation
Feel awkward, slowly retreat because you feel you're losing private space

questions.question: Your general level of trust in your partner is...

options.text: High, I trust them until proven otherwise
Fluctuating, I am often suspicious and need constant reassurance
Low, I find it hard to fully trust anyone but myself

questions.question: When your partner wants to spend time with their friends without you, you...

options.text: Fully support it, having your own social life is healthy
Feel jealous and abandoned, 'why not take me?'
Feel relieved, that means you also have time for yourself

questions.question: How do you express love?

options.text: Openly and naturally, I easily say 'I love you'
Very intensely, but I always demand the same affirmation back
Verbally difficult, I prefer to keep a safe distance

questions.question: When a relationship ends, your main reaction is...

options.text: Sad, process the heartbreak appropriately, then move on
Shattered, feel like the world is ending, and beg to get back together
Act cold, move on quickly, and convince yourself you're fine

questions.question: In your opinion, emotional dependence on a partner is...

options.text: Normal within healthy limits (interdependent)
Very important, I need them to feel whole and secure
A sign of weakness, I refuse to depend on anyone

questions.question: When your partner is very sad or stressed, you...

options.text: Listen, be emotionally present, and offer support
Panic a lot, get stressed too, and feel it might be your fault
Feel awkward and prefer to give them space

questions.question: When you are the one feeling down, you hope your partner will...

options.text: Be by my side and willing to listen to my complaints
Drop everything to comfort me right away
Not interfere too much, I can handle it myself

questions.question: How do you define an ideal relationship?

options.text: Two independent individuals who complement each other and grow together
Two souls completely merged with no secrets
Two people living together but maintaining absolute independence

questions.question: When you feel doubtful or insecure in a relationship, you...

options.text: Talk about it honestly and openly with your partner
Become very clingy and seek excessive validation
Withdraw emotionally and prepare for the worst-case scenario

questions.question: What is your biggest fear in a romantic commitment?

options.text: Being in an unhealthy relationship full of unresolved conflict
Being left, rejected, or replaced by someone else
Losing freedom, being controlled, and losing your identity

questions.question: If your partner wants to discuss marriage/serious future plans...

options.text: Respond enthusiastically and realistically
Very happy because it's the security I've been wanting
Feel pressured, avoid, or try to change the topic

questions.question: If you have a secret or past trauma, you...

options.text: Will tell it naturally when you feel safe enough
Tell everything early so they won't leave you later
Keep it tightly sealed, no one needs to know your weaknesses

questions.question: When your partner is quiet and looks sullen for no apparent reason...

options.text: Ask them gently if they want to talk
Panic and assume you've made a fatal mistake
Leave them alone, can't be bothered with emotional drama

questions.question: How do you feel when your partner gives excessive compliments?

options.text: Happy and genuinely grateful
Happy but often doubt if they're being honest
Feel awkward, cringey, and uncomfortable

questions.question: Regarding your partner's phone password privacy...

options.text: We know each other's passwords but never snoop because we trust each other
I must know to make sure they're not cheating
That's absolute privacy, I don't want to know and they can't know mine

questions.question: A friend says your partner is very attractive and many people like them...

options.text: Proud because you are the one they chose
Immediately feel insecure and monitor their movements extra tightly
Don't care, if they want to cheat then go ahead

questions.question: Your partner gets a big promotion that makes them much busier...

options.text: Support their career and find new ways to manage time
Worried they will find someone better and leave you
Actually glad because you can have more time to yourself

questions.question: When jealousy strikes, your natural response is...

options.text: Communicate honestly with them without accusing
Get angry, sulk, or make sarcastic remarks so they realize
Suppress the feeling deeply and act cold towards them

questions.question: When you are invited to meet their immediate family for the first time...

options.text: Enthusiastic and prepare calmly
Very stressed, afraid they won't like you and ruin your relationship
See it as a burden and look for excuses to postpone

questions.question: If you disagree about weekend hobbies...

options.text: Compromise or occasionally do your own hobbies separately
I'll go along with their hobby even if I don't like it, as long as we're together
Just do your own thing, I don't want the hassle

questions.question: When your partner gives constructive criticism about your attitude...

options.text: Evaluate yourself and accept it if it's true
Feel attacked, unloved, and immediately feel worthless
Get angry, shut down, and retaliate by criticizing their flaws

questions.question: When you really need help...

options.text: Ask them directly without guilt
Give strong hints and get angry if they're not sensitive enough to help
Try hard to solve it yourself without involving them

questions.question: How often do you think about the status of your relationship?

options.text: Just the right amount, I enjoy the process as it goes
Almost all the time, I often analyze their every move
Rarely, I have many other focuses in life

questions.question: Your view on 'Me Time' in a relationship...

options.text: A healthy need to maintain balance
Feel it's a threat, like they want to distance themselves from me
An absolute need, I could go crazy if we have to stick together all the time

questions.question: If your partner says, 'I need some time alone' during an argument...

options.text: Give them space and agree to discuss it later
Panic intensely and force them to resolve the issue right away
Go ahead, I'd actually prefer if we don't have to discuss it again

questions.question: When there's a problem, you more often...

options.text: Call or meet directly for clear communication
Send long text messages full of emotion and demands
Read their message and leave them on read until you're in the mood

questions.question: If an ex suddenly contacts you...

options.text: Tell your current partner to avoid misunderstandings
Panic and hide it because you're afraid your partner will get angry and leave
Delete it quietly, that's my personal business and they don't need to know

questions.question: Seeing a very independent and successful partner makes you...

options.text: Admire them and feel lucky to have them
Afraid they will realize they don't actually need me
Very relieved because they surely won't be a burden to me

questions.question: Public displays of affection (PDA) in your opinion...

options.text: Fine as long as it's within the bounds of decency
Very important to show the world that we belong to each other
Very uncomfortable and excessive

questions.question: Responsibility for happiness in the relationship...

options.text: We both try to create that happiness together
It's my partner's job to make me feel secure and happy
I am only responsible for my own happiness

questions.question: Your partner forgets to celebrate your anniversary...

options.text: A little disappointed but remind them jokingly
Cry and feel it's definite proof they don't love me anymore
No problem at all, I also hate troublesome celebrations

questions.question: When you strongly disagree with your partner's big decision...

options.text: State your logic clearly while still respecting their choice
Reluctantly agree and give in for fear of triggering a big fight
Ignore it and secretly do what you think is right

questions.question: Your view on absolute loyalty...

options.text: It's the foundation of mutual respect
It's the promise that makes me most anxious if broken
It's just a concept that often limits one's freedom

questions.question: Your partner has a close friend of the opposite sex...

options.text: It's fine, as long as they know the professional/friendship boundaries
Very jealous, always suspicious, and often check their closeness
Good, so there's someone else to deal with them when they're being annoying

questions.question: When you're in a long-distance relationship (LDR) or rarely meet...

options.text: Keep in touch reasonably and stay focused on your activities
Tormented by longing and demand calls every moment to feel secure
Actually feel more comfortable because you don't have to meet every day

questions.question: If the relationship starts to feel flat and boring...

options.text: Take the initiative to invite your partner to try new activities together
Panic and overthink that their love is fading
Naturally start looking for other activities or new friends outside

questions.question: About lending a very valuable item/large sum of money to your partner...

options.text: Help them if the reason is rational and it won't hurt my finances
Will definitely give it even if I struggle, so they know my sacrifice
Refuse firmly, romance and finances must be completely separate

questions.question: About changing your habits/appearance for your partner...

options.text: Only willing to change if it makes me a better person
Ready to change myself 180 degrees as long as they keep liking me
Strongly refuse, take it or leave it

questions.question: How often does the thought 'they will leave me' cross your mind?

options.text: Rarely, I'm quite confident in our relationship's foundation
Very often, it's a nightmare that always haunts my mind
Don't care, if they want to leave then go ahead, I can live alone

questions.question: In terms of apologizing after a big fight...

options.text: I'm not ashamed to apologize first if I was indeed wrong
I often apologize even when not wrong, just so they're not angry anymore
I find it very hard to apologize and prefer to give silent treatment for days

questions.question: When your partner cancels a plan because they suddenly got sick...

options.text: Understand, offer help, or visit them
Suspect whether they're really sick or just making excuses to avoid me
Alright, I'll immediately make fun plans with my friends

questions.question: If you have to move to a faraway city for your career...

options.text: Discuss it thoroughly together to find a win-win solution
Willing to sacrifice my career as long as we don't have to separate
Leave immediately, career is my priority, love comes later

questions.question: Seeing parents or friends divorce/break up badly...

options.text: Take it as a lesson to be better at communicating
Become more anxious and afraid the same thing will surely happen to me
Reinforce my belief that commitment is nonsense that restricts

questions.question: The worst thing a partner could do to you is...

options.text: Lie and manipulate reality
Leave you without a word (Ghosting)
Restrict you, be blindly jealous, and secretly read your phone

questions.question: To you, intimacy is...

options.text: A place where two people can be vulnerable without fear of judgment
An absolute sanctuary where I finally feel completely accepted
A dangerous area that can be used to hurt me
